headline: General Motors CONFIRMED at 40.6 Functional — May 11-12 IT layoffs documented. GM laid off 500-600 IT workers (Austin TX, Warren MI) framed as 'skills swap' toward AI-native roles. Laid-off employees reported 'ominous email' and severance concerns (CNBC May 12). This is the latest in 18-month white-collar layoff series. Composite at 40.6 (0.6 above Functional floor at 40.0) — boundary watch active. Layoff event alone is sub-threshold for material proposal; documented as B1 self-sustainability + B3 scope clarity signal. Confirm at 40.6 Functional with internal dimensional pressure -2.5 (sub-threshold).
boundary_watch_status: Active; 0.6 above Functional floor (40.0). Internal reconstructed dimensional pressure: -2.5 (sub-threshold). Carry-forward DOWNWARD pressure logged per v1.2 (rule is bilateral — applies to both upward and downward sub-threshold pressure).

internal_reconstructed_dimensions_with_c.carry_forward_disposition: Per v1.2 Sustained-Conduct Rule (bilateral application), -2.5 dimensional pressure preserved as documented downward pressure. Future positive evidence could offset; future negative evidence could push to threshold for material proposal.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 unchanged. Laid-off employees reported 'ominous email' — communication-channel deficiency, no positive AWR change.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5 unchanged at published level. Internal pressure -0.1 from severance-concerns reporting (CNBC).
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3 unchanged. Layoff execution itself is operational action, not compassion-relevant change.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 unchanged. No EQU-relevant change in window.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3 unchanged at published level. Internal pressure -0.2: 'skills swap' framing for cost-cutting layoffs documents B3 Scope Clarity gap.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 unchanged at published level. Internal pressure -0.1 from severance-concerns reporting.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 unchanged. AI-native repositioning is structural, but no SYS-dimension reform signal.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5 unchanged at published level. Internal pressure -0.1: 'skills swap' vs. actual cost-cutting reality.

key_evidence: GM laid off 500-600 IT workers (May 11-12, 2026) primarily in Austin TX and Warren MI.
Layoffs framed as 'skills swap' toward AI-native roles.
Laid-off employees reported 'ominous email' and concerns about severance (CNBC May 12).

evidence.finding: 500-600 IT workers laid off; primary locations Austin TX and Warren MI.

evidence.source: CNBC — GM layoffs AI severance May 12
evidence.finding: Laid-off employees report 'ominous email' and severance concerns; AI-skills-swap framing.

math_check.validator_check: Confirmation at 40.6 Functional. Internal downward pressure -2.5 logged per v1.2.

negativeSignals: 500-600 IT layoffs; 'ominous email' communication pattern; severance concerns reported; 18-month layoff series cumulative; 'skills swap' framing inconsistent with cost-cutting reality.
sector_context: US Auto / labor-welfare cluster. GM's boundary placement (0.6 above Functional floor) makes future negative evidence likely band-crossing material.
open_actions_carried: Watch: severance practice outcomes for laid-off workers
Watch: AI-substitution narrative vs. cost-cutting reality
methodology_consistency_check: v1.2 bilateral application: downward dimensional pressure preserved as carry-forward. Confirmation at published score. Methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
